title: GoHighLevel 30-Day Free Trial, Promo Codes & Discounts 2026: Every Way to Save
description: The real answer on GoHighLevel promo codes, discounts, and free trials in 2026. Get 30 days free (double the standard 14), annual savings tips, Extendly add-on options, and every FAQ answered. No fake coupon codes.
html_content:
The standard GoHighLevel trial is 14 days. That's not enough time to properly evaluate a platform this deep. By the time you've set up your CRM, built a funnel, and tested a workflow or two, your trial is already over. The good news: there's a way to get 30 days instead of 14 — and it costs you nothing extra.

I'll walk you through exactly how to get the extended trial, what you get access to during those 30 days, and what happens when the trial ends so there are no surprises.

Get 30 Days Free — Double the Standard Trial

Full platform access. No credit card required. No strings.

Start Your 30-Day Free Trial

What's in This Guide

  • How to Get 30 Days Instead of 14
  • Step-by-Step Trial Setup
  • What's Included During Your Trial
  • How to Maximize Your 30 Days
  • What Happens When Your Trial Ends
  • FAQ

How to Get 30 Days Instead of 14

GoHighLevel's default trial through their main website is 14 days. The extended 30-day trial is available through specific partner links. When you sign up through our extended trial link, you get the full 30 days automatically. There's no promo code to enter, no hidden step. The extended duration is applied at signup.

This isn't a limited-time offer or a hack — GoHighLevel makes the 30-day trial available through their affiliate and education partners because they know the platform has a learning curve. More time means more people actually experience the value before the trial ends. It's a smart move on their part, and it works in your favor.

One thing to be clear about: the 30-day trial gives you the exact same access as a paid account. You're not getting a watered-down version. Every feature, every tool, every integration — it's all there from day one.

Step-by-Step Trial Setup

Setting up your GoHighLevel trial takes about 5 minutes. Here's exactly what the process looks like:

SETUP WALKTHROUGH

Step 1: Click the 30-day trial link. You'll land on the GoHighLevel signup page with the extended trial already applied.

Step 2: Enter your name, email, and create a password. No credit card is required at this stage. You won't be charged anything to start.

Step 3: Verify your email. GHL sends a confirmation link — click it to activate your account.

Step 4: Complete the onboarding questionnaire. GHL asks about your business type and goals to customize your dashboard. Answer honestly — it helps surface the right features first.

Step 5: You're in. Your 30-day clock starts now. You have full access to the CRM, funnels, workflows, calendars, email marketing, and everything else.

Step 6: Set up your first sub-account if you're planning to manage clients. Import your contacts, connect your domain, and start building. Don't wait — the 30 days go fast when you're actually using the platform.

What's Included During Your Trial

The trial gives you full access to the platform. Here's what that actually means in practical terms:

CRM and Pipeline Management: Import contacts, create custom fields, build pipelines with drag-and-drop stages, and manage your entire sales process from one place. The CRM is the backbone of GHL and you'll want to set this up first.

Funnel and Website Builder: Build landing pages, multi-step funnels, and full websites using the drag-and-drop editor. Templates are included, but honestly, start from scratch — the templates are generic. You'll build something better.

Workflow Automations: This is where GHL earns its keep. Build multi-step automations triggered by form submissions, tag changes, calendar bookings, pipeline stage moves, or just about any event in the system. The workflow builder is visual and surprisingly powerful.

Email and SMS Marketing: Send broadcasts, create drip campaigns, and use automations to send targeted messages. Note that SMS and email have usage costs (~$0.015/text, ~$0.01/email) even during the trial, so start small while you're testing.

Calendar and Booking: Create booking calendars, set availability rules, and embed scheduling widgets on your site or funnels. This alone replaces Calendly for most people.

Reputation Management: Send review requests, monitor Google reviews, and manage your online reputation from the dashboard.

AI Features: Conversation AI, AI-powered content creation, and AI employees are accessible during the trial. AI usage is metered, so you'll see how the costs stack up before committing.

Integrations: Connect to Stripe, Mailgun, Twilio, Google, Facebook, and dozens of other platforms. The Zapier and Make integrations work during trial too.

How to Maximize Your 30 Days

30 days sounds like a lot, but if you don't have a plan, you'll burn through it exploring menus and watching tutorials without building anything real. Here's the order I'd recommend:

30-Day Game Plan

Week 1: Set up your CRM, import contacts, connect your domain, and build your first funnel or landing page. Get the foundation in place.
Week 2: Build 2-3 workflows — a lead capture automation, a follow-up sequence, and a pipeline stage trigger. This is where you'll see the real value.
Week 3: Set up calendars, test SMS and email campaigns with a small batch, and explore the AI features. Start tracking costs.
Week 4: Evaluate. Are the tools replacing other software you're paying for? Does the workflow builder handle your use cases? Run the numbers and decide on a plan.

The biggest mistake I see people make is treating the trial like a demo. It's not. It's 30 days of full access to a platform that replaces 10+ tools. Use it like a paying customer from day one, and you'll know exactly whether it's worth the investment by week four.

What Happens When Your Trial Ends

When your 30-day trial expires, your account is paused — not deleted. Your data, funnels, workflows, contacts, and everything you built stays intact. You just can't access it until you choose a paid plan.

GoHighLevel gives you a window after trial expiration to subscribe without losing anything. You won't be auto-charged because no credit card was required to start. You'll get email reminders as your trial winds down, and when it ends, you simply pick a plan to reactivate.

Pricing starts at $97/month for the Starter plan, $297/month for Unlimited (most agencies pick this one), and $497/month for SaaS Pro. Annual billing saves about 17% if you're ready to commit. For the full pricing breakdown, check out our GoHighLevel Pricing 2026 guide.

Ready to See What GoHighLevel Can Do?

30 days. Full access. No credit card. No risk.

Start Your 30-Day Free Trial

Standard 14-Day Trial vs Our 30-Day Trial

GoHighLevel's default signup is a 14-day trial. Through the partner link above, you get 30 days. Everything else about the trial is identical — same features, same access, no credit card required. The only difference is time.

  Standard GHL Trial Extended Trial (Our Link)
Trial Length14 days30 days
Credit Card RequiredNoNo
Feature AccessFull platformFull platform
Auto-Charge After TrialNoNo
Extra Time to Build Real AutomationsBarelyYes — 16 more days

16 extra days sounds small but changes everything. Most people spend week 1 just navigating the UI. With 14 days, that leaves a week to actually build — not enough to test meaningful workflows. With 30 days, you have 3 full weeks of real usage after orientation.

Who This 30-Day Trial Is Built For

The extended trial is most valuable for people evaluating GHL as their primary business tool — not tire-kickers exploring out of curiosity. Specifically:

Marketing agencies considering GHL to replace 5-10 separate SaaS tools (HubSpot, ActiveCampaign, Calendly, ClickFunnels, etc.). You need time to migrate actual client workflows and stress-test them.

Service businesses (home services, professional services, coaches, consultants) wanting a CRM + automation + booking stack in one place. Your calendar, SMS reminders, and lead follow-up live here.

SaaS entrepreneurs exploring the SaaS Pro plan to rebill GHL as your own white-labeled platform. You need the time to configure sub-accounts, snapshot templates, and a pricing layer.

Small business owners hearing GHL is an "all-in-one" and wanting to verify before committing $97-$497/mo. The 30 days lets you actually use it like a paying customer.

The Truth About GoHighLevel Promo Codes

Let me save you 20 minutes of Googling: there is no working GoHighLevel promo code for a discount on your monthly subscription. Every "coupon" site you've landed on is either outdated, fake, or redirecting you to the same standard signup page with no discount applied.

GoHighLevel does not distribute public coupon codes for ongoing subscription discounts. Their pricing is set — Starter at $97/month, Unlimited at $297/month, SaaS Pro at $497/month — and they don't undercut it with floating promo codes. This is actually a good sign. Companies that constantly discount are usually struggling. GHL's retention speaks for itself.

That said, GHL isn't rigid. There are legitimate ways to reduce your cost — they're just not structured as "enter SAVE20 at checkout" gimmicks. The real options are: the 30-day extended trial (already covered above), annual billing savings, and seasonal promotions.

Annual Billing Savings (~17% Off)

Once you're past the trial and committed to GHL, switching to annual billing is the most straightforward way to reduce ongoing cost. GoHighLevel offers approximately 17% off when you pay annually instead of monthly.

Plan Monthly Annual You Save
Starter$97/mo~$970/yr (~$81/mo)~$194/yr
Unlimited$297/mo~$2,956/yr (~$246/mo)~$608/yr
SaaS Pro$497/mo~$4,950/yr (~$413/mo)~$1,014/yr

The savings are meaningful, especially on higher tiers. On SaaS Pro, annual billing saves over $1,000 per year. My advice: don't switch on day one. Use the 30-day trial, go month-to-month for 2-3 months while still evaluating, then switch to annual once you're confident GHL is your long-term platform. No penalty for switching from monthly to annual at any point.

Seasonal Promotions (Black Friday & Summer)

GoHighLevel runs seasonal promotions a couple times per year, typically around Cyber Monday/Black Friday and during summer. These are the only times GHL offers direct discounts on subscription pricing.

Typical seasonal deal: 50% off for the first 3 months. On Starter, that's $48.50/month instead of $97 for your first three months. On Unlimited, $148.50/month instead of $297. After three months, pricing returns to standard.

Timing Tip

If you're reading this close to Black Friday or summer, it might be worth waiting for a seasonal deal. But if you're months away, don't sit on your hands — the 30-day free trial plus annual billing saves you more than the seasonal promo in most scenarios. Start building now and switch to annual when you're ready.

Seasonal promotions are announced through GHL's email list and social channels. They don't require a coupon code — discounted pricing is applied directly during the promotional window. Be on their email list and you'll catch it when it happens.

Why Coupon Aggregator Sites Are Wasting Your Time

If you've searched "GoHighLevel promo code" or "GHL coupon," you've probably landed on sites like RetailMeNot, CouponBirds, or a dozen copycat pages claiming to have working codes. Those sites scrape coupon codes from across the internet and list them regardless of whether they work.

Most GoHighLevel "codes" on those sites are completely fabricated, expired years ago, or just redirect you to the standard GHL signup page with no discount. The sites make money from affiliate commissions when you click through — they don't care whether the "code" actually saved you anything.

Here's the tell: check the comments section on any of those coupon pages. You'll find people saying "didn't work," "expired," or "just took me to the regular page." GoHighLevel's pricing is set. They don't distribute subscription discount codes through third-party sites. The only legitimate discounts are the three covered in this guide.

The Best Deal Available Right Now

If you're looking to save money on GoHighLevel today, here's the play:

Step 1: Start with the 30-day extended free trial. A full month of free access — more valuable than any percentage-off code.

Step 2: Use those 30 days to actually build something. Import your contacts, create workflows, test the features. Make an informed decision.

Step 3: When you're ready to subscribe, go month-to-month for a couple months to confirm it's the right fit.

Step 4: Switch to annual billing once committed. The 17% discount compounds year after year.

Combined, this approach saves more than any promo code would. You get a full free month upfront plus ongoing annual savings. On Unlimited, that's roughly $456 saved in your first year compared to someone who started with the standard 14-day trial on monthly billing.

Don't Want to Set It Up Yourself? Extendly Handles GHL Onboarding For You

If you want the 30-day trial but don't want to spend those 30 days doing the setup yourself, Extendly does it for you. They handle GoHighLevel onboarding, 24/7 white-label support for your clients, and pre-built snapshots you can import and run. We use them ourselves.

WHAT EXTENDLY DOES FOR YOU

  • Full GoHighLevel onboarding and setup
  • 24/7 white-label support your clients contact directly
  • Pre-built snapshots ready to import and run
  • Saves you 30-60 hours of platform configuration
Check out Extendly →

Affiliate disclosure: if you sign up through this link, we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need a credit card to start the free trial?

No. GoHighLevel does not require a credit card to start the trial. You sign up with your name and email, verify your email address, and you're in. You won't be charged anything until you actively choose a paid plan after the trial ends.

Can I cancel during the trial?

Yes. Since no credit card is required, you can simply stop using the platform and your trial will expire on its own. There's nothing to cancel because there's no payment method on file. If you want to formally close the account, you can do that from your settings.

Can I switch plans during the trial?

The trial gives you access to the full platform. When your trial ends and you choose a paid plan, you select the tier that fits your needs at that point. You're not locked into anything during the trial period.

Is the 30-day trial really free?

The platform access is completely free for 30 days. The only costs you might incur during the trial are usage-based charges if you send SMS messages (~$0.015/text) or emails through the platform (~$0.01/email). You can avoid these by not sending messages during your evaluation, or keep them minimal by testing with small batches.

What if I need more than 30 days?

30 days is the maximum trial length available. If you need more time, your best option is to start on the Starter plan at $97/month — you can cancel anytime if you decide GHL isn't the right fit. Your trial data carries over to the paid account seamlessly.

Is there a GoHighLevel promo code?

No. GoHighLevel does not issue subscription discount codes. Every "promo code" you see on third-party coupon sites is either fake, expired, or a redirect to the standard signup page with no discount applied. The real ways to save are the 30-day extended trial, annual billing (~17% off), and seasonal promotions run directly by GHL.

Is there a GoHighLevel discount code?

No public discount codes exist. GHL's pricing is set at $97/$297/$497 per month and not discounted via codes. The legitimate ways to reduce cost: 30-day extended trial via partner link, 17% annual billing savings, or waiting for a seasonal promo (Black Friday/Cyber Monday, summer).

Is there a GoHighLevel coupon code?

No working GoHighLevel coupon codes exist. Coupon aggregator sites list codes that don't work. The closest thing to a coupon is the 30-day extended free trial — 16 extra days of free access, which is worth more than any percentage-off code would give you.

How long is the GoHighLevel free trial?

Standard signup through GHL's website gets you 14 days. Through partner links (including the one on this page), you get 30 days. That's the maximum trial length available. Both versions give you full platform access with no credit card required.

Is GoHighLevel free? Is there a free account?

No. There is no permanent free tier or free account. GoHighLevel is a paid platform starting at $97/month. The only free access available is the 30-day extended trial — full features, no credit card, 30 days. After that, you choose a paid plan to continue.

What's the difference between the 14-day and 30-day GoHighLevel trial?

Only the length. Both are full-platform access with no credit card required and no auto-charge when the trial ends. GoHighLevel's default signup offers 14 days; partner links (like the one on this page) apply a 30-day version automatically at signup. No promo code to enter, no hidden step.

Can I get GoHighLevel cheaper than $97/month?

Not on monthly billing. Starter at $97/month is the floor. To pay less per month, you switch to annual billing — Starter drops to roughly $81/month when billed annually. That's about $970 per year instead of $1,164. No promo codes or negotiations get you below the standard monthly rate outside of seasonal promotions.

What is the best GoHighLevel deal right now?

The best deal is the 30-day extended free trial followed by annual billing once you're committed. Combined, you get a full free month upfront plus ~17% off the annual rate. On Unlimited, that's roughly $456 saved in year one vs standard 14-day monthly billing. No coupon code required — both discounts are live right now.

How do I get a GoHighLevel discount?

Three ways. (1) Start with the 30-day extended trial — 16 extra days of free access. (2) Switch to annual billing once you're a paying customer — saves ~17%. (3) Watch for seasonal promotions (Black Friday, summer) for 50% off first 3 months. There are no other discount paths.

Does GoHighLevel do Black Friday deals?

Yes. GoHighLevel has historically run Black Friday and Cyber Monday promotions, typically offering 50% off for the first 3 months. These are announced through GHL's official email list and social media — no coupon code required, discounted pricing is applied directly during the promotional window.

Do GoHighLevel affiliate links give a discount?

Affiliate links don't reduce subscription price, but most affiliate partners (including us) offer the extended 30-day trial instead of the standard 14 days. That extra 16 days of free access is worth more than any typical percentage-off code.

Are there GoHighLevel student or nonprofit discounts?

GoHighLevel does not advertise specific student or nonprofit discounts. Best path for both groups: the 30-day extended trial followed by the Starter plan at $97/month (or ~$81/month with annual billing). For nonprofit use cases, contact GHL support directly — they may be able to work something out.

GoHighLevel vs HubSpot free — which for agencies?

HubSpot's free tier is real but severely limited — essentially contact storage with minimal automation. GHL has no free tier but delivers a full agency CRM plus funnels, automations, calendars, email/SMS, and reputation tools. For an agency, GHL's paid plan replaces 5-10 tools you'd otherwise pay for separately, which usually nets out cheaper than HubSpot-plus-other-SaaS stacks.

GoHighLevel free trial duration 2026 — is it still 30 days?

Yes. As of 2026, the extended 30-day trial is still available through partner links. GoHighLevel has not changed the partner-link trial length. The standard 14-day trial on their main site remains 14 days.
